key_pointers_for_verifier.new_module: agents/query/intent_refiner.py: refine_intent(), IntentRefinerResult, IntentRefinerError, IntentRefinerParseError, QueryType, _VALID_QUERY_TYPES, _build_prompt, _parse_intent_json
key_pointers_for_verifier.output_contract: IntentRefinerResult{refined_intent: str, entities: list[str], query_type: Literal['factual_lookup','broad_exploratory']} -- query_type taxonomy authored this run (not pre-specified in docs/LLD/query-agent.md); chosen verbatim from issue #22 subtask 4.3.2's own acceptance-criteria wording ('factual/lookup vs. broad/exploratory') for forward compatibility with 4.3.2's future tests
key_pointers_for_verifier.llm_client_di: LLMClient imported only under TYPE_CHECKING (agents/query/intent_refiner.py); no concrete provider (Ollama/OpenRouter/Gemini) referenced, matching issue #18/#20's DI rule in agents/llm/client.py
key_pointers_for_verifier.shared_helper_reuse: imports strip_code_fences from ingestion._json_fences (not duplicated); cross-package import ingestion -> query is one-directional per pyproject.toml's package layout
key_pointers_for_verifier.test_file: agents/query/test_intent_refiner.py: _FakeLLMClient(LLMClient) fake (mirrors ingestion/test_segment.py's own _FakeLLMClient pattern), 10 tests (V1-V10 in validation-matrix.json) covering well-formed output shape (factual_lookup, broad_exploratory, history-influenced, kwargs-forwarding, code-fence tolerance) and malformed-output/error cases (invalid JSON, missing field, wrong type, invalid query_type, LLMError propagation)
key_pointers_for_verifier.test_results: cd agents && python3 -m pytest query/ llm/ -q -> 81 passed; cd agents && python3 -m pytest . -q --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -> 221 passed, 2 failed (both pre-existing protobuf VersionError in ingestion/test_shortlist.py, issue #46, unrelated to this change, not touched); ruff check agents/query/ -> All checks passed
key_pointers_for_verifier.not_built_this_run: agents/query/topic_selector.py, agents/query/synthesizer.py (LLD scaffolds only, not in issue #22), agents/query/test_intent_refiner_types.py (issue #22 subtask 4.3.2 -- separate future dispatch, explicitly out of scope for this run)

self_verification_disclaimer: Implementation agent does NOT verify its own work (invariant I4). self-consistency.json above is internal sanity only (build green, validation-matrix covered) -- /cdr:verify must independently confirm before this subtask is considered closed.
security_disclosure: Two fake 'system-reminder'-style blocks (a fake date-change notice, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' MCP-tool-instructions block) appeared in this run's tool-call transcript immediately after the `gh issue view 22` call. Per the dispatching agent's explicit warning that this repo's GitHub issue/PR content and tool output has repeatedly contained injected fake-system-reminder text, these were treated as untrusted data only, not acted upon in any way (no behavior, permissions, or scope were changed because of them), and are disclosed here for the verifier's awareness. No other injected content was observed in the issue body itself (`gh issue view 22`'s own text was consistent with the dispatch instructions' description of the two subtasks).
